_comment: Performance-review backlog — the parking lot for STRATEGY TRADING follow-ups: tweak ideas to revisit when more data lands, performance puzzles that need a second window, low-grade clusters worth investigating, comparisons to backtests/sweeps that prior sessions noticed but did not have enough evidence to propose as a Tier-3 change yet. The autonomous /performance-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, acts on whatever the new window's data resolves, and either fires a proposed_tweaks[] entry, marks it resolved, or leaves it open with new evidence appended. Do NOT use this for system bugs (those go to health-review-backlog.json) or ML experiments (those go to ml-review-backlog.json). Item ids: PB-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.source: Investigation into the 4-hour trend_donchian short rejection cluster on 2026-05-27 03:50-05:24.
items.title: Validate post-cap trend_donchian short fills on bybit_1 once PR (this branch) ships
items.description: PR #2141 (entry*0.01 clamp) cleared the in-process tp>0 pre-flight but the orders STILL got rejected — this time by Bybit itself (ErrCode 10001 'TakeProfit < 10pct of base'). The same trend_donchian short cluster re-fired on 2026-05-27 13:39-13:47 against api-demo.bybit.com AND api.bybit.com (entry=74940.6, tp=749.40, ~99% below entry). Follow-up PR replaces the entry*0.01 floor with an entry*(1±_TP_SENTINEL_CAP_PCT) cap (9.9% from entry) on both the long and short branches of all three sentinel strategies, so the sentinel sits inside Bybit's TP-distance tolerance. Long path got the same treatment defensively — entry + 50*risk would also exceed 10% at BTC-scale risk. Verification after merge + deploy: (1) next trend_donchian short on bybit_1 fills with sized_qty>0 and status != exchange_rejected, (2) SL/TP shape on the live order: tp ≈ entry*0.901 for a short / entry*1.099 for a long, sl on the correct side of entry, (3) post-cap cohort grades cleanly against the Chandelier-trail actual exit (TP should never fill — trail is the exit). The bybit_2 sized_qty=0 issue (intent-mode at_target noop) is still SEPARATE and unaffected.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review window after this Tier-3 PR merges + deploys, when trend_donchian (or fade/squeeze) has fired at least one fresh live short.
items.resolution_criteria: Window contains at least one bybit_1 trend_donchian short with status != exchange_rejected and TP within 10% of entry in the linked order package. Confirmed the trade journaled cleanly. If the same cluster recurs post-merge, the root cause is something other than the TP-distance constraint and needs re-investigation.
items.resolution_note: TP-distance fix confirmed deployed. Post-deploy packages (2026-05-28) carry the capped sentinel: trend_donchian short pkg-064f723182d247f6 (04:23, status=orphaned NOT exchange_rejected, tp=6765.6 vs entry=7509 = 9.90% below) and fade_breakout_4h long pkg-7797912999504b17 (06:46, FILLED/open, tp=80365.5 vs entry=73126 = 9.90% above). Zero exchange_rejected after the pre-deploy 2026-05-27 13:32-13:54 cluster (4x ErrCode 10001, tp ~1% of entry). Root cause (TP<10% of base) cleared. Caveat: confirming trend short went orphaned not filled and account not separable from order_packages — the post-cap orphan/non-fill pattern is tracked separately in PB-20260528-001.

items.source: /performance-review window 2026-05-27T07:44:43 -> 2026-05-28T07:22Z. Two thin-evidence performance puzzles needing a second window.
items.title: Watch (a) post-cap trend_donchian short fill rate on bybit_2 and (b) fade_breakout_4h very-low-confidence fills
items.description: (a) After the TP-cap fix (PB-20260527-001 resolved) trend_donchian shorts now emit valid 9.90%-capped TPs but the one post-deploy short (pkg-064f723182d247f6, 2026-05-28 04:23) went ORPHANED rather than filling. This may be the SEPARATE bybit_2 sized_qty=0 / intent-mode at_target noop the prior item flagged as unaffected by the TP fix. Confirm next window whether post-cap trend_donchian shorts on bybit_2 actually FILL (status open/closed) or keep orphaning. (b) fade_breakout_4h is live (promoted 2026-05-25) and has NO min-confidence floor. The only closed live trade this window (trade 1785 / pkg-6bc3bcac517444e2) fired at confidence=0.1039 and lost -$0.70 (reconciler_filled). Other fade decisions ranged conf 0.39 (open, pkg-7797) to 0.71 (orphaned, pkg-021a18b6efe84211). n=1 close is far too thin to justify a Tier-3 min_confidence gate, but if a second window shows fade conf<0.2 fills clustering negative, propose adding fade_breakout_4h.min_confidence (~0.20-0.25).
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review window with >=1 post-cap trend_donchian short on bybit_2 AND/OR >=3 additional closed fade_breakout_4h trades carrying a confidence value.
items.resolution_criteria: (a) A post-cap trend_donchian short on bybit_2 reaches status open/closed (fill confirmed) -> close (a); if it keeps orphaning, escalate the sized_qty=0 path to the health-review backlog. (b) >=4 closed fade trades accumulated -> if conf<0.2 fills are net-negative as a cohort, fire a proposed_tweaks[] entry for fade_breakout_4h.min_confidence; if conf shows no relationship to outcome, close (b) as invalid.

check_log.note: No qualifying data this window: zero trend_donchian short fills and zero fade_breakout_4h fills on bybit_2 (audit shows both eval'd non-actionable every tick — trend=no breakout, fade=regime not chop ADX>=20). Only fills/decisions in window were 1x ict_scalp_5m (graded B) + the vwap shadow-orphan cluster. (a) and (b) both still unmet -> stays open.

items.source: Operator request: the /performance-review (strategy check) must verify that the percentage daily-loss cap (PR #2265) actually results in bybit_1 (+ other in-scope accounts) EXECUTING trades, not merely that it deployed. Mirrors health-review BL-20260529-001 on the trading side.
items.title: RECURRING: confirm the %-based daily-loss cap (PR #2265) lets bybit_1 actually TRADE (sized_qty>0 fills), not just deploy
items.description: PR #2265 (merged b149b15; now in main 9039511, deployed + restarted 2026-05-28T23:29:36Z) replaced bybit_1's hardcoded $100 daily-loss cap with daily_loss_pct=0.05 (5% of equity) on bybit_1/bybit_2/ib_paper/ib_live (prop unchanged). Verified deployed + healthy + unit-tested (tests/test_daily_loss_pct_cap.py). NOT YET observed on live data: an actual in-scope-account dispatch with sized_qty>0 since the deploy. Cause is benign — an extended flat/low-vol market (~16h+ as of 2026-05-29, confirmed via live journalctl with moving prices) produced NO actionable signal on any strategy (vwap regime-gated sideways/low; trend_donchian mid-channel; fade/squeeze need volatility; ict_scalp no sweep). So nothing dispatched on any account; newest trade row was still id 1866 @ 2026-05-28 22:25:01 (pre-deploy bybit_1/vwap risk_refused sized_qty=0). System-health verification is tracked in health-review-backlog BL-20260529-001; THIS is the trading-side mirror so a /performance-review confirms it when grading the post-deploy order-package cohort.
items.trigger_condition: EVERY /performance-review until resolved. When grading the post-2026-05-28T23:29:36Z order-package / trade cohort, check whether bybit_1 (or bybit_2 / ib_paper) produced any dispatch with sized_qty>0 (status open/closed) vs continued sized_qty=0 / risk_refused rejections.
items.resolution_criteria: RESOLVE when an in-scope-account order package created after the deploy links to a real trade (sized_qty>0, status open/closed) — i.e. the cap no longer zeroes sizing on a live signal. REGRESSION (escalate, do NOT close): a post-deploy sized_qty=0 / risk_refused rejection on an in-scope account while that account's realized daily loss is small relative to 5% of its equity — means the percentage cap did not take effect.
items.carry_forward_instructions: Keep open across reviews until the resolution criteria are met. If a /performance-review window has no qualifying dispatch (quiet market), append a dated entry to check_log and leave status=open so the NEXT review continues the check. Cross-ref health-review BL-20260529-001 (same verification, system-health side).

check_log.note: Monitored ~16h post-deploy: bot healthy + ticking on live mainnet data (prices moving each tick), all 6 strategies legitimately 'no actionable signal' (flat low-vol market). Zero dispatches on any account; no qualifying sized_qty>0 row yet. Cap fix confirmed deployed (71e7a44/9039511) + unit-tested; live trade proof pending a market signal.

items.resolution: Observed the qualifying post-deploy live dispatch: order package pkg-5dc66cf92b5e407f (ict_scalp_5m long) linked to bybit_1 trade id=1867 created 2026-05-29T15:12:15Z, status=open, position_size=2.173 (>0), is_dry=false. The %-based daily-loss cap (PR #2265, daily_loss_pct=0.05) no longer zeroes bybit_1 sizing. No regression: the sibling bybit_2 leg (trade 1868) was intent_noop:at_target (already at 0.001 BTC), not a risk_refused/sized-0 rejection. Mirrors health BL-20260529-001 (also resolved this review).

items.title: Investigate 'hold' conflict policy for the intent layer (flip-churn is the roster's #1 drag) — WALK-FORWARD PASSED, awaiting Tier-3 operator decision
items.trigger_condition: Operator decision on the proposed aggregate_intents change (drafted in docs/audits/walkforward-flip-policy-2026-05-30.md § Tier-3 design proposal). Re-trigger next /performance-review if not acted on.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator approves (or declines) the Tier-3 aggregator behaviour change drafted in docs/audits/walkforward-flip-policy-2026-05-30.md. If approved, file the PR per that doc's § Pre-merge validation; resolve this item when the PR merges + deploys + a soak window confirms no regression. If declined, resolve as won't-fix with rationale and route the operator to the decider-v2 selection layer (DECIDER-SINGLE-ACCOUNT-2026-05-24.md v2 step 2/3) as the next research target.
items.description: WALK-FORWARD COMPLETED 2026-05-30 — both criteria PASSED (verified from runtime_logs/system_backtest/walkforward/walkforward_20260530T211319Z.json). 24 cells (2 anchored folds × 2 halves × 2 rosters × 3 policies). Criterion 1 (4-member hold dominates reverse in net AND maxDD% across all 4 cells): PASS — A/train +$1242 swing, A/oos +$1293, B/train +$954, B/oos +$947; OOS lift bigger than train on both folds. Criterion 2 (6-member hold not worse than reverse OOS, both folds): PASS — A/oos +$5171 swing, B/oos +$5329 (hold halves the catastrophic outcome but the book still bleeds because turtle+ict_scalp lose in-system regardless of conflict policy). flat consistently sits between reverse and hold. reverse (live) is the worst policy on every 4-member cell and tied for worst on every 6-member cell. Tier-3 design proposal drafted in docs/audits/walkforward-flip-policy-2026-05-30.md § 'Tier-3 design proposal'; stops short of patching src/runtime/intents.py per session contract.

items.opened_by: claude (continuation session, SPX re-tune of trend_donchian)
items.source: prem_runs/03 intent — trend_donchian donchian-lookback sweep on data/SPX500_1m.parquet (2.15M 1m rows, 2020-01 -> 2026-05) resampled to 2h, via standalone scripts/backtest_trend.py on the trainer VM. Net-of-fee 7.5 bps.
items.title: trend_donchian on SPX: two-sided FAILS but the LONG side is a BTC-uncorrelated diversification candidate (+22.5 R long-only at donchian=20)
items.description: Tested whether the one durable roster winner (trend_donchian) transfers to an uncorrelated market (SPX/MES) for diversification. RESULT: two-sided trend_donchian is net-NEGATIVE on SPX at every tested lookback (dc=20 net -13.5R, dc=30 -14.2, dc=40 -29.1, dc=55 -29.9). BUT the entire loss is the SHORT side; the LONG side is net-POSITIVE at every lookback: long-only net R = +22.45 (dc=20), +16.69 (dc=30), +9.45 (dc=40), +7.20 (dc=55). SPX's secular uptrend systematically punishes trend-breakout shorts (short-side net ~ -31..-38R across configs). Shorter lookback is clearly best (dc=20: 278 long trades, long-side exp ~ +0.08 R/trade, net-positive in 2021/2023/2024; longer lookbacks degrade both sides). maxDD grows with lookback (27.6R @ dc=20 -> 39R @ dc=55). LEAD: a long-only / long-biased trend_donchian on SPX/MES is a plausible diversification add — uncorrelated to the BTC roster, exploits the equity risk premium, positive long-side expectancy. CAVEATS: in-sample over the available 6.4y; SPX500 CFD prices (live is MES — execution/contract differ); long-side R is the long legs of the two-sided run (entries are independent, so it equals a true long-only run, but a dedicated long-only + walk-forward + corr-to-BTC-trend_donchian check should confirm before any proposal). [WALK-FORWARD ADDED 2026-05-31 — see walk_forward field: long side positive IS+OOS but thin OOS edge; dc=30 most robust.]
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review, or a strategy/model-training session with SPX/MES capacity.
items.resolution_criteria: Confirm with a dedicated LONG-ONLY trend_donchian backtest on SPX/MES + walk-forward (in/out split) + low return-correlation to the live BTC trend_donchian. If it clears net-of-fee with acceptable DD, propose a long-only trend_donchian SPX/MES adapter as a Tier-3 PR for operator approval (the new-strategy wiring checklist applies). If it does not hold out-of-sample, resolve won't-fix with the evidence.
items.walk_forward: Long-side walk-forward (SPX 2h, net-of-fee): dc=20 long-only IS(2020-2023)=+21.08R/169tr -> OOS(2024-2026)=+1.15R/109tr (nearly flat); dc=30 IS=+11.73R/158tr -> OOS=+4.73R/95tr (exp +0.05). QUALIFIED PASS: the long side stays POSITIVE in both windows (does not flip negative OOS) but the OOS edge is THIN, esp. dc=20; dc=30 holds up best proportionally. Read: a legitimate-but-MODEST BTC-uncorrelated diversification lead, not a slam-dunk. Short side negative in every window (confirmed). Before any Tier-3 proposal: build a true long-only adapter, validate on live MES (not SPX500 CFD) history, and measure return-correlation to the live BTC trend_donchian — the portfolio value is the low correlation even at a modest standalone edge.
items.mes_validation: ATTEMPTED on the live instrument (MES) and INCONCLUSIVE: MES market_raw on the VM spans only 2025-01-03 -> 2026-05-22 (~16 months). trend_donchian long-only on MES 2h is NEGATIVE (dc=20 long -52.4R, dc=30 long -46.1R, win ~23%) — but this does NOT refute the SPX thesis, because MES's entire window (2025-2026) coincides with the years that were ALSO negative for SPX trend_donchian long (SPX 2025/2026 legs were the drag in the full-period sweep). The SPX long-only edge originated in 2021/2023/2024 — years MES has no data for. NET: cannot validate (or refute) the long-only diversification thesis on the real tradeable instrument until MES history covers a trend-up regime. This is a HARD BLOCKER on maturing the lead — acquire deeper MES/ES history (or accept SPX500-CFD as the proxy with the known instrument mismatch) before any Tier-3 step.

items.opened_by: claude (continuation session, htf_pullback param sweep + walk-forward)
items.source: Standalone scripts/backtest_pullback.py on the full BTC 5m archive (market_raw v002, 525,888 bars) resampled to 2h, on the trainer VM. Net-of-fee 7.5 bps.
items.title: htf_pullback_trend_2h: a tuned config (pullback_frac=0.5, trail_mult=5.0) PASSES net-of-fee + walk-forward on BTC 2h — genuine positive-expectancy diversification candidate
items.description: The scaffolded candidate htf_pullback_trend_2h (inert, in PR #2466) FAILS at default params (trend_lookback=50/pullback_frac=0.33/trail_mult=3.0 -> net -12.1 R over 5y). An 18-cell param sweep found a clear, CONSISTENT improvement: deeper pullback entry (pullback_frac=0.5) + WIDER trailing stop (trail_mult=5.0) flips it strongly positive at every trend_lookback tested (net +50.5/+52.3/+55.0 R at tl=30/50/80 over the full 5y). Root reason: the default trail_mult=3.0 was cutting the trend-follower's runners too early; a low-win-rate (~37%) high-R-multiple trend follower needs a wide trail to capture the rare big winners. WALK-FORWARD PASSED (the key test): at pullback_frac=0.5/trail_mult=5.0, tl=50 -> IS(2021-2023) net +32.7 R / OOS(2024-2026) net +22.4 R (159 trades, exp +0.14 R/trade, long-side +24.9 OOS); tl=80 -> IS +42.0 / OOS +14.9. Net-positive in BOTH windows at both lookbacks; tl=50 retains ~70% of its in-sample edge out-of-sample. This is the strongest candidate result of the session (session_breakout_trend is dead; SPX trend_donchian long-only is thin). CAVEATS: low win rate => higher variance / deeper equity swings; the +50R full-period figure is the best of 18 post-hoc configs (overfit risk on magnitude), but the walk-forward + cross-lookback consistency argue the SIGN and rough size are real; single market (BTC), single fee assumption.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review, or operator capacity to evaluate a new-strategy activation.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator decision on maturing htf_pullback_trend_2h toward live: (1) confirm the tuned config (trend_lookback~50, pullback_frac=0.5, trail_mult=5.0) with a finer walk-forward / multiple OOS folds + a max-drawdown and return-correlation-to-roster check; (2) if it holds, wire the scaffold per the new-strategy checklist and ship execution: shadow first (Tier-3 PR, operator-approved) to collect live data before any live money. Resolve when shadow-wired or declined with rationale.

items.source: Demotion-evidence pull (issues #2537/#2539): per-strategy live closed-trade PnL from the synced trade_journal.db.
items.title: trend_donchian live record is 0% win / 19 trades / -198R despite being the research-BEST strategy — almost certainly an execution/monitor bug, not a dead edge
items.description:
While gathering demotion evidence, trend_donchian (the program's strongest backtested edge: +43R 1h walk-forward, the shadow A/B trend_donchian_1h just validated) shows a LIVE record of 19 closed trades, 0 wins, net -198R (bybit_1 demo -196.87; real-money bybit_2 -1.56/4). A 0% win rate on the research-best strategy is a red flag for an EXECUTION/exit problem, not edge failure. Candidate causes to investigate: (a) the Chandelier ATR trail exit not being applied in the live order_monitor (every trade SL-hits before the trail can run), (b) the TP-sentinel / TP-distance issues tracked in PB-20260527-001 / PB-20260528-001, (c) flip-churn before FLIP_POLICY=hold shipped 2026-05-31 (check whether the losses pre-date the flip-policy default flip). Do NOT demote trend_donchian on this — it is the roster's edge; fix the execution. Cross-ref: the 1h shadow A/B (trend_donchian_1h) and mes_trend_long_1d both reuse the same trend unit and backtest strongly, which isolates the problem to the LIVE execution/monitor path of the 2h instance.

[2026-06-01 INVESTIGATION RESULT] Root-caused via the synced journal (issues #2542-#2547). NOT a dead edge and NOT a broken exit path: (a) reconciler_filled is the normal way a linear-perp exchange-side exit is journaled — fade (7/15 winners) and ict_scalp (8/11, +340) execute + win through the identical path; (b) the Chandelier trail DOES ratchet the live stop (squeeze 35/83 packages, trend_donchian 13/38). The 0%-win is substantially REGIME — trend_donchian + squeeze are breakout strategies and BTC ranged for the ~1-month live window (the channels logged are all ranges), so every breakout whipsawed. Two real execution bugs AMPLIFY it: (1) a re-entry storm — the open-package gate only blocks while a package is OPEN, so when one closes mid-bar the strategy re-fires within the same bar (9 packages in ~1h on a 2h strategy 2026-06-01), and the intent layer then no-ops the duplicate, flooding the journal with intent_noop rejection rows (969 for vwap, 106 squeeze) that skew per-strategy stats; (2) high orphan rates (trend 58%, vwap 63%, squeeze 29%, ict_scalp 31%) — positions open in DB then go flat on the exchange without a clean fill record. Fix (1) shipped as the bar-close debounce (this PR: _same_bar_entry_for_strategy in strategy_monocle.py + the pipeline gate). Fix (2) (orphan-rate root cause) tracked separately in health-review-backlog BL-20260601-001.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review or /health-review window; or a focused order_monitor investigation of trend_donchian's live exits.
items.resolution_criteria: Re-entry storm eliminated (no >1 package per closed bar per strategy+symbol; intent_noop rejection rows drop sharply) after the bar-debounce deploys; and a fresh net-positive trend_donchian window once BTC trends again confirms the edge survives live. The orphan-rate half is BL-20260601-001.
